verificationNote: Risk matrix constructed for cloud computing environments based on ISO 27005, NIST SP 800-30, and cloud security best practices. Band actions are indicative -- adjust thresholds and escalation paths to your organisation's risk appetite and regulatory context.
title: Cloud Security Risk Matrix (5x5)
description: Standard 5x5 risk matrix with cloud-specific band definitions and actions. Impact is weighted toward data exposure, business continuity, and regulatory compliance. Band actions reference NACSA Act 854 and PDPA notification obligations where applicable.

scale.label: Rare
scale.cloudContext: Requires sophisticated nation-state capability targeting specific CSP infrastructure; no known precedent in the organisation's cloud environment; CSP has strong isolation controls with no public bypass

scale.label: Unlikely
scale.cloudContext: Requires targeted attack with insider knowledge; CSP has compensating controls; few sector precedents; limited internet exposure of the specific service

scale.label: Possible
scale.cloudContext: Known attack patterns exist (credential stuffing, misconfiguration exploitation); CSP advisories issued for components in use; industry peers have experienced similar incidents

scale.label: Likely
scale.cloudContext: Common misconfiguration pattern; automated scanning tools detect the vulnerability class; public S3 bucket / open storage incidents are frequent across industry; weak IAM policies in use

scale.label: Almost Certain
scale.cloudContext: Internet-exposed services with default credentials or no authentication; publicly known API keys; storage with public read access; no MFA on privileged accounts; active exploitation campaigns

scale.label: Negligible
scale.dataExposure: No sensitive data exposed; internal metadata only
scale.businessContinuity: Momentary disruption (<1 hour); no business impact
scale.reputational: No public awareness; internal incident only

scale.label: Minor
scale.dataExposure: Internal non-sensitive data exposed; no PII or regulated data
scale.businessContinuity: Short disruption (1-8 hours); limited business impact; workaround available
scale.regulatory: No mandatory notification; internal compliance documentation required
scale.reputational: Limited awareness; contained to affected parties

scale.label: Moderate
scale.dataExposure: Limited PII exposure (<1,000 records) or non-critical business data; single cloud service affected
scale.businessContinuity: Extended disruption (8-72 hours); significant business impact; degraded operations
scale.regulatory: PDPA notification may be required; BNM RMiT incident reporting triggered for financial sector

scale.label: Major
scale.dataExposure: Large-scale PII exposure (>1,000 records) or sensitive business data; multiple cloud services affected; cross-account compromise
scale.businessContinuity: Prolonged disruption (>72 hours); critical business processes impacted; customer-facing services down
scale.regulatory: Mandatory PDPA notification; NACSA s26 notification if NCII-designated; potential regulatory investigation
scale.financial: Remediation cost USD 1,000,000 - 10,000,000; potential regulatory fines
scale.reputational: National media coverage; customer trust erosion; potential customer loss

scale.label: Catastrophic
scale.dataExposure: Massive data breach (>100,000 records); complete cloud estate compromise; regulated data exfiltrated at scale
scale.businessContinuity: Extended outage with no clear recovery path; complete loss of cloud services; business survival threat
scale.regulatory: Multiple regulatory investigations; NACSA enforcement action; potential operating licence impact
scale.financial: Remediation cost exceeding USD 10,000,000; regulatory fines; litigation
scale.reputational: International media coverage; severe and lasting reputational damage; executive accountability

bands.action: Accept with documented rationale. Monitor during periodic risk review. No immediate action required.
bands.cloudNote: Even low-rated risks involving regulated data (PII, financial, health) should be documented for compliance purposes.

bands.action: Mitigate within 90 days. Implement cloud-native security controls. Track in risk register with assigned owner. Verify CSP-side controls are adequate.
bands.cloudNote: Medium risks on internet-facing cloud services should be prioritised for mitigation within 30 days due to automated scanning and exploitation speed.

bands.action: Mitigate within 30 days. Implement interim compensating controls immediately. Report to CISO and cloud governance board. Include in next NACSA s21 risk assessment update if NCII-designated.
bands.cloudNote: High risks with data exposure potential require immediate access restriction. Consider quarantining affected cloud resources pending mitigation.

bands.action: Immediate remediation within 24 hours. Board-level escalation required. If data breach confirmed, initiate PDPA notification process. For NCII-designated operators, NACSA s26 notification within 6 hours if incident is confirmed.
bands.escalation: CEO / Board / NACSA (if NCII-designated) / PDPA Commissioner (if data breach)
bands.cloudNote: Critical risks may require immediate revocation of compromised credentials, isolation of affected cloud accounts, and engagement of CSP incident response support. Document all actions for regulatory reporting.

complianceOverride.description: Compliance override rule: any risk involving confirmed non-compliance with PDPA, BNM RMiT, or NACSA Act 854 is automatically elevated to High band minimum regardless of likelihood score. Regulatory non-compliance cannot be accepted.
complianceOverride.rule: IF regulatoryNonCompliance == true THEN band >= High
complianceOverride.rationale: Regulatory non-compliance carries mandatory reporting obligations, potential fines, and reputational damage that cannot be offset by low likelihood. Data sovereignty violations and missing mandatory controls are compliance risks by definition.
